title: fix: prevent git merge conflicts during cache updates
body:
## Summary

Fixes git merge conflicts that occur when updating the local git cache during Flutter SDK installations.

## Problem

Users were experiencing merge conflicts when installing Flutter versions 3.27.1+ with git cache enabled:

```
error: Your local changes to the following files would be overwritten by merge:
        engine/src/flutter/bin/et.bat
Please commit your changes or stash them before you merge.
Aborting
```

## Solution

Added git cleanup commands before fetch operations to ensure a clean working directory:

- `git reset --hard HEAD` - Discards any local changes
- `git clean -fd` - Removes untracked files and directories

These commands run before `git fetch` operations to prevent merge conflicts.

## Changes

### Core Fix
- **lib/src/services/git_service.dart**: Added git cleanup before fetch operations

### Developer Experience Improvements
- **pubspec.yaml**: Added Husky and lint_staged for automated code quality
- **.husky/pre-commit**: Runs formatting, fixing, and analysis on staged files
- **.husky/pre-push**: Runs tests before pushing
- **Optimized configuration**: Simplified lint_staged for better performance

## Testing

✅ **Manual Testing**: Created reproduction script that confirms the fix works
✅ **Unit Tests**: All existing tests pass (188 passed, 1 skipped)
✅ **Integration Tests**: Comprehensive Flutter SDK installation tests
✅ **Git Hooks**: Pre-commit and pre-push hooks working correctly

## Impact

- **Fixes**: Git merge conflicts during Flutter SDK installations
- **Improves**: Developer experience with automated code quality checks
- **Maintains**: Backward compatibility with existing workflows

## Closes

Closes #819

---

### Verification

To verify the fix:

1. Enable git cache: `fvm config --use-git-cache`
2. Install a Flutter version: `fvm install 3.27.4`
3. Create conflicts in git cache manually
4. Install another version - should work without merge conflicts

The fix ensures that any local changes in the git cache are automatically cleaned before fetch operations, preventing the merge conflict scenario entirely.

---
Pull Request opened by [Augment Code](https://www.augmentcode.com/) with guidance from the PR author

fix(frontend): Fix builder UI glitch
body: There are a few UI bugs on the builder that this PR addresses. ### Changes 🏗️ Fix these UI issues: * (screenshot attached above) Key-value input width was unintentionally maxed out due to a stale CSS rule. * When multiple executions within the same node are running, we pick the latest status, making one running and one completed execution displayed as completed. * No balance errors were executed, only displayed while at least one node execution was triggered, while this can be done directly when the execution request is triggered. * Run & Stop button glitch: it's still showing as stopped when the graph is still running, this is due to way the UI code tracks execution in the node-level, instead of graph level. ### Checklist 📋 #### For code changes: - [x] I have clearly listed my changes in the PR description - [x] I have made a test plan - [x] I have tested my changes according to the test plan: - [x] Manual tests on the described behaviours.

title: Rework history
body:
My [previous implementation](https://github.com/SimonHalvdansson/Harmonic-HN/commit/167851f3598ecdc48d53b8880ea8e1f238a88554) of history doesn't have any sort mechanism. I think it's better to sort the history by clicked time. So the last clicked item is on top.

Hence, it required saving the time when the story is clicked by user.

Also the time shown on `meta_view` is not time from api response but using the time when the story is clicked.

![Screenshot_20250519_123153](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df272753-3037-4c43-96fb-9132c6dfa5b7)

The way history is saved to sharePrefs is similar to Bookmark.

```xml
<string name="com.simon.harmonichackernews.KEY_SHARED_PREFERENCES_HISTORIES">
44023680q1747630184892-44025484q1747630183027-44024759q1747630177525-44020591q1747630175592
</string>
```

title: Replace markdown issue templates with YAML versions for bug reports, …
body:
This pull request updates the issue templates in the `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE` directory to replace the old markdown-based templates with new YAML-based forms. The new templates provide a more structured and user-friendly experience for submitting bug reports, feature requests, and map/layer requests. Key changes include adding validation rules, placeholders, and dropdowns for better guidance, as well as introducing a new template for map/layer requests.

### Bug report template updates:
* Replaced the markdown-based `bug_report.md` with a YAML-based `bug_report.yml`, adding structured fields for environment details, steps to reproduce, and relevant logs. The new form includes a quick self-triage checklist to help users troubleshoot common issues before submitting.
* 
### Feature request template updates:
* Replaced `feature_request.md` with `feature_request.yml`, introducing a structured format for describing the problem, ideal solution, and alternatives considered. A checklist ensures users verify the feature isn't already possible or requested. 

### New map/layer request template:
* Added a new `map_request.yml` template to facilitate requests for new maps, tilesets, or data packages. Includes fields for source details, coverage area, license information, and the value of the request to users.

title: fix(dashboard): update LEGACY_DASHBOARD_URL handling and add documentation in .env.example
body:
This pull request introduces changes to improve the configuration and environment variable handling for the legacy dashboard URL. The updates ensure better flexibility and compatibility for deployment environments by adding support for overriding the legacy dashboard URL via a global variable.

### Configuration Updates:

* [`apps/dashboard/src/config/index.ts`](diffhunk://#diff-bd0ee084bc8c9f5eb4441d4351c95cdbb10fc1d4af72e2646db6c3297b9c397bL27-R28): Updated `LEGACY_DASHBOARD_URL` to allow fallback to a global variable (`window._env_?.VITE_LEGACY_DASHBOARD_URL`) if it is defined, otherwise defaulting to the existing environment variable (`import.meta.env.VITE_LEGACY_DASHBOARD_URL`). This improves flexibility in how the URL is configured.

### Environment Variable Documentation:

* [`docker/community/.env.example`](diffhunk://#diff-d0d70d9482de238e804a52bd8ab90525a61a85152c649a1ddff6dc52c86ddfa4R51): Added a comment to document the purpose of the `VITE_LEGACY_DASHBOARD_URL` variable, specifying that it should point to the host and port of the web container. This helps clarify its usage for developers and users.

### Docker Configuration:

* [`docker/community/docker-compose.yml`](diffhunk://#diff-3553fc766756fb977edd9560e25cd13d2f8e0eb268a1f91760ad298482766b75R171): Added `VITE_LEGACY_DASHBOARD_URL` to the `services` environment variables, ensuring it is properly passed to the container during deployment.

# Fix unreachable allowSubdomains code in crawler filterURL method

## Summary

Fixed a bug where the `allowSubdomains` feature in the Firecrawl crawler was completely non-functional due to unreachable code. The subdomain check logic was placed after an early return statement that would deny all external links, making it impossible to execute.

**Key Changes:**
- Moved subdomain check logic from after the final external link denial to before it in the `filterURL` method
- Added comprehensive test cases for `allowSubdomains` functionality 
- Preserved all existing logic and behavior for non-subdomain cases

The `allowSubdomains` option was already properly configured through the API pipeline but the core filtering logic was unreachable, making the feature completely broken.

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] **Verify control flow logic**: Review the `filterURL` method in `crawler.ts` to ensure subdomain check happens at the correct point in the decision tree
- [ ] **Test with real subdomains**: Test crawling a website with actual subdomains (e.g., `blog.example.com`, `api.example.com`) with `allowSubdomains: true` and verify they are crawled
- [ ] **Verify no regressions**: Test normal crawling without `allowSubdomains` to ensure existing functionality is unchanged
- [ ] **Test edge cases**: Try combinations like social media subdomains, external links + subdomains, malformed URLs
- [ ] **Run full test suite**: The local test environment had configuration issues preventing full test execution

**Recommended test plan:** Start a crawl of a domain with known subdomains (like `firecrawl.dev`) with `allowSubdomains: true` and verify the results include subdomain URLs.

---

### Diagram

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[crawl.test.ts]:::major-edit --> B[WebCrawler.filterURL]
    B --> C[Social Media Check]:::context
    C --> D[External Domain Excludes]:::context
    D --> E[External Content Links]:::context
    E --> F[Subdomain Check]:::major-edit
    F --> G[Final External Link Denial]:::context
    
    H[crawler.ts]:::major-edit --> B
    I[types.ts allowSubdomains]:::context --> B
    J[crawl-redis.ts config]:::context --> B
    
    subgraph Legend
        L1[Major Edit]:::major-edit
        L2[Minor Edit]:::minor-edit  
        L3[Context/No Edit]:::context
    end
    
    classDef major-edit fill:#90EE90
    classDef minor-edit fill:#87CEEB
    classDef context fill:#FFFFFF
```

### Notes

- The `isSubdomain` helper method and `allowSubdomains` configuration were already properly implemented
- This fix only required moving existing logic to the correct position in the control flow
- Local testing confirmed the server accepts crawl requests with `allowSubdomains` parameter
- Test environment configuration issues prevented full test validation

Link to Devin run: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/54ced5143c834d499640a33019d406ce

**Requested by:** Micah Stairs (micah@sideguide.dev)

Skip payout pause for sellers with accounts older than 2 years
body:
# Skip payout pause for sellers with accounts older than 2 years

## Summary

Adds an early return condition to `pause_payouts_for_seller_based_on_recent_failures!` method to skip automatic payout pausing for sellers whose accounts were created more than 2 years ago. This addresses an issue where VIP sellers (typically established, high-volume sellers) were having their payouts incorrectly paused due to legitimate high transaction volumes triggering the fraud detection system.

**Changes:**
- Added single line early return: `return if seller.created_at < 2.years.ago`
- Added comprehensive test coverage for different account age scenarios
- Tests verify sellers >2 years are protected, =2 years still get flagged, <2 years work normally

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] **Verify business logic threshold**: Confirm whether accounts exactly 2 years old should be protected (current logic uses `<` so they still get flagged)
- [ ] **Test null safety**: Verify behavior when `seller` is nil (Purchase model shows `optional: true` - this could cause crashes)
- [ ] **Edge case testing**: Test accounts created exactly 2 years ago vs 2 years + 1 day to verify boundary conditions
- [ ] **End-to-end verification**: Test with actual purchase failure scenarios to ensure established sellers are protected while fraud detection still works for newer accounts
- [ ] **Monitor production impact**: After deployment, monitor for any unintended consequences on existing seller populations

**Recommended test plan**: Create test scenarios with sellers of different account ages and high failure volumes to verify only newer accounts get payouts paused.

---

### Diagram

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Purchase Model] --> B[mark_failed! transition]
    B --> C[pause_payouts_for_seller_based_on_recent_failures!]:::major-edit
    C --> D{Feature active?}
    D -->|No| E[Return early]
    D -->|Yes| F{Error code ignored?}
    F -->|Yes| E
    F -->|No| G{Seller account > 2 years old?}:::major-edit 
    G -->|Yes| E
    G -->|No| H[Check failed purchase thresholds]
    H --> I{Threshold exceeded?}
    I -->|Yes| J[Pause payouts + Add comment]
    I -->|No| K[No action]
    
    L[blockable_spec.rb]:::major-edit
    
    subgraph Legend
        L1[Major Edit]:::major-edit
        L2[Minor Edit]:::minor-edit  
        L3[Context/No Edit]:::context
    end
    
    classDef major-edit fill:#90EE90
    classDef minor-edit fill:#87CEEB
    classDef context fill:#FFFFFF
```

### Notes

- **Context**: Requested after VIP sellers (Sam9y, Hypermatic, David Barnett) had payouts incorrectly paused
- **Risk**: Could not test locally due to database connectivity issues - CI and manual testing critical
- **Business impact**: Protects established sellers from false positives while maintaining fraud protection
- **Implementation**: Simple but high-impact change to fraud detection system
- **Potential null pointer**: Need to verify seller is always present when this method is called

Link to Devin run: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/70fe9192a6f94fec8445a7bdbf11afb5
Requested by: Ershad Kunnakkadan (hello@ershadk.com)

title: Allow billing multiple project- and hourly-rates per invoice
body:
Extracted out from #360.

Focuses only on making invoices more dynamic and allowing contractors to bill multiple flexible rates per invoice. The contractor-specific changes will be made in #360 after this is merged. The alert when a contractor is billing more than their default rate will be added separately as well.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f24756cf-9e48-43e1-88b8-18da210bc478)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Unified invoice line item model now uses "quantity" and an "hourly" flag, replacing previous "minutes" and "total amount" fields.
  - Invoice forms and tables now consistently display and handle "Hours / Qty" for all line items.
  - Added a new `QuantityInput` component for entering quantities or durations with hourly indication.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Simplified invoice calculations and validations, reducing errors related to mixed hourly and project-based billing.

- **Refactor**
  - Removed all project-based contractor logic and minute-based tracking from invoices.
  - Updated database schema and API to support the new quantity-based invoicing model.
  - Streamlined invoice creation, editing, and presentation logic to use the unified quantity/hourly approach.
  - Simplified invoice rejection emails and consolidated invoice receipt views to match new model.

- **Style**
  - UI labels and table headers updated to reflect "Hours / Qty" terminology.

- **Tests**
  - Test suites revised to align with the new quantity-based invoice model, removing project-based and minute-based test cases.
  - End-to-end tests updated to remove time input interactions and reflect new quantity-based fields.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: chore(v2): release solstatus 2.0.0-beta.0
body:
## 2.0.0-beta.0 (2025-06-22)

### Updates

* IaC via Alchemy
* Monorepo via pnpm
  * App and Api services now have separate dependency trees and bundles
* UI improvements, especially to the endpoint monitor page
* Performance improvements (which also means reduced costs!)
* Biome v2
* Incremental cache
* Many small bug fixes

### Upgrade from v1.7.x to v2.0.x

It is advisable to read the updated README.md first to get familiar with the new repo structure. That said, updating is an easy two step process

1) Rename the following Cloudflare resources:
        - Worker: `monitor-exec-production` -> `solstatus-prod-monitor-exec`
        - Worker: `monitor-trigger-production` -> `solstatus-prod-monitor-trigger`
        - Worker: `solstatus-production` -> `solstatus-prod-app`
2) Run `pnpm cli --fqdn uptime.example.com --stage prod` (with your actual FQDN)

body:
## Why is this change needed? This PR implements the `generateUsecaseNode` functionality to generate use cases from analyzed requirements using AI. The implementation follows the same structured output pattern as other agents in the codebase. The `generateUsecaseNode` uses `QAGenerateUsecaseAgent` to generate structured use cases based on the requirements analyzed by `analyzeRequirementsNode`, formatting them according to the `usecaseSchema`. ```ts // Single usecase schema const usecaseSchema = v.object({ // TODO: Replace with IDs (UUID) when DB is implemented requirementType: v.picklist(['functional', 'non-functional']), // Type of requirement requirementCategory: v.string(), // Category of the requirement requirement: v.string(), // Content/text of the specific requirement title: v.string(), description: v.string(), }) // Response schema for structured output const generateUsecasesResponseSchema = v.object({ usecases: v.array(usecaseSchema), }) export type Usecase = v.InferOutput type GenerateUsecasesResponse = v.InferOutput< typeof generateUsecasesResponseSchema > ``` ⚠️ I haven't included node retry logic in this pull request. Since I've skipped some tests, please let me address the retry mechanism in a separate PR, along with those tests. ## What would you like reviewers to focus on? ## Testing Verification I confirmed that the workflow works correctly in the local environment. Langfuse log https://cloud.langfuse.com/project/cm8ii4o5o03fpad078o638g1d/traces/e9643e94-5549-460d-91a3-7dfc451c80af?timestamp=2025-06-23T07:00:55.880Z&display=details trigger.dev log ![ss 3464](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/001b180a-c364-4c32-8fab-1094884ff01b) UseCase was like this: ```json [ "generateUsecaseNode" ]"Usecase 1 (functional)":{ "requirementType":"functional", "requirementCategory":"Profiles", "requirement":"Provide individual profiles for students and teachers containing relevant personal and professional information", "title":"User Profile Creation and Update", "description":"A student or teacher logs into the platform for the first time and is prompted to complete a profile. The user uploads a photo, writes a bio, and for teachers adds subjects taught, qualifications, and hourly rate. The system validates mandatory fields and proper formatting, displaying inline messages for any errors. Once all information is correct, the user saves the profile, the system stores the data, and the completed profile page is shown. At any later time, the user can open “Edit Profile,” modify details, and save changes, which the system immediately reflects." } ``` ## What was done ### 🤖 Generated by PR Agent at a54185a3c09dd6b15e1846b3761c38b109c03cca - Implement `generateUsecaseNode` with AI-powered use case generation - Add `QAGenerateUsecaseAgent` for structured use case creation - Update workflow state to include generated use cases - Add comprehensive error handling and logging functionality ## Detailed Changes

Restore NotoSerifJP and NotoSansJP in Playground Font Settings
body:
### Summary  
This PR restores **NotoSerifJP** and **NotoSansJP** in the font settings of the playground by uncommenting their values.  

### Related Discussion  
This issue was discussed in [GitHub Discussions #845](https://github.com/pdfme/pdfme/discussions/845).  

### Why This Change Is Important  
Restoring Japanese fonts makes it easier to confirm that **pdfme supports non-English fonts**, which is valuable for library selection.  
In my case, I used the playground to **demonstrate pdfme’s usability** to our sales team.  
After deciding to adopt pdfme and starting development, we noticed that Japanese fonts were missing, which caused confusion.  

### What I Did  
- Uncommented **NotoSerifJP** and **NotoSansJP** in the playground’s font settings.  

### Verification  
- Confirmed that this change **does not affect e2e tests**.  

This is a simple fix, but it improves usability for users who need **multilingual PDF support**.  
Thank you for your time and consideration! 😊

title: Remove redundant validation check in invoice service
body:
# Remove redundant validation check in invoice service

## Summary

This PR fixes HTTP 422 errors when submitting invoices by removing a redundant validation check in `CreateOrUpdateInvoiceService`. The service was performing an `all_values_present` check that duplicated model-level validations, causing generic "Please input all values" errors instead of the more specific model validation messages like "Invoice line items description can't be blank".

**Changes made:**
- Removed lines 16-21 in `CreateOrUpdateInvoiceService` containing the redundant `all_values_present` validation
- Updated test expectations in shared example group to expect the actual model validation error message
- Both RSpec test failures are now resolved

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] **Verify model validations are sufficient** - Confirm that `InvoiceLineItem` model validations properly handle all the validation cases that were previously covered by the service-level check
- [ ] **Test invoice submission end-to-end** - Submit an invoice with missing/empty description field via the frontend to ensure the error message is user-friendly and actionable
- [ ] **Check for other dependencies** - Search codebase for any other places that might expect or handle the "Please input all values" error message
- [ ] **Verify error message consistency** - Ensure the new error message "Invoice line items description can't be blank" matches the pattern of other validation errors in the system

**Recommended test plan:** Try submitting the original failing invoice payload `{"invoice":{"invoice_date":"2025-06-26"},"invoice_line_items":[{"description":"-","pay_rate_in_subunits":1200000,"quantity":1,"hourly":false}]}` and verify it now returns a helpful error message instead of HTTP 422 with generic text.

---

### Diagram

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[CreateOrUpdateInvoiceService.rb]:::major-edit --> B[Invoice Model]:::context
    A --> C[InvoiceLineItem Model]:::context
    D[create_or_update_invoice_service_spec.rb]:::major-edit --> A
    E[invoices_controller.rb]:::context --> A
    F[Frontend Invoice Submission]:::context --> E
    
    B --> G[ActiveRecord Validations]:::context
    C --> H[description presence validation]:::context
    
    subgraph Legend
        L1[Major Edit]:::major-edit
        L2[Minor Edit]:::minor-edit  
        L3[Context/No Edit]:::context
    end
    
    classDef major-edit fill:#90EE90
    classDef minor-edit fill:#87CEEB
    classDef context fill:#FFFFFF
```

### Notes

- The Playwright test failure in CI is unrelated to this change (tax settings UI issue)
- This change should improve user experience by providing more specific error messages
- The removed validation was checking `item.values.all?(&:present?)` which was too broad and prevented proper model validation errors from surfacing

title: ✨(app): add idempotency to chat API endpoint
body:
## Issue - resolve: N/A ## Why is this change needed? Currently, the chat API endpoint can process duplicate requests if called multiple times with the same payload. This PR adds idempotency key generation to prevent duplicate task execution in Trigger.dev, ensuring that identical chat requests are only processed once. ## What would you like reviewers to focus on? - The idempotency key generation logic using SHA-256 hash of the entire payload - Integration with Trigger.dev's idempotencyKeys SDK - Removal of timestamp and TTL as requested to handle cases with tens of seconds of delay ## Testing Verification - Manual testing: Confirmed that reloading multiple times without a completed response from the LLM does not trigger ## What was done ### 🤖 Generated by PR Agent at 6514b232ef0fbe53e8a9d74a6b911ada651edd8d - Add idempotency key generation to chat API endpoint - Prevent duplicate chat request processing using SHA-256 hash - Integrate Trigger.dev SDK for idempotency management - Update package dependencies for idempotency support ## Detailed Changes

title: feat: implement complete PostgreSQL schema deparser with indexes and constraints
body:
## Issue

- resolve: https://github.com/route06/liam-internal/issues/4976

## Why is this change needed?

The current PostgreSQL schemaDeparser implementation only generates CREATE TABLE statements. This PR adds support for indexes and constraints to match the functionality of operationDeparser.

## What would you like reviewers to focus on?

- Correctness of DDL generation for indexes and constraints
- Proper ordering of DDL statements (especially foreign key constraints)
- Test coverage completeness

## Testing Verification

All tests pass successfully:
- 17 test cases covering basic tables, indexes, constraints, complex schemas, and error handling
- Tests verify that generated SQL is parseable by the PostgreSQL parser
- No breaking changes to existing functionality

## What was done

### Added Index Generation
- CREATE INDEX statements for all table indexes
- Support for UNIQUE and non-UNIQUE indexes
- Support for index types (BTREE, etc.)
- Support for composite column indexes

### Added Constraint Generation
- ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT statements for all constraint types
- Support for PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, and CHECK constraints
- Foreign key constraints are added last to ensure referenced tables exist
- Proper handling of ON UPDATE/DELETE options for foreign keys

### Improved Error Handling
- Try-catch blocks for each DDL generation
- Errors are collected but don't stop processing
- Clear error messages identifying the problematic table/constraint

### Added Comprehensive Tests
- Test cases for all index variations
- Test cases for all constraint types
- Complex schema tests with multiple tables and circular references
- Error handling validation

## Additional Notes

This implementation follows the same patterns and utilities used in operationDeparser.ts for consistency.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Expanded PostgreSQL schema export to include CREATE INDEX and ADD CONSTRAINT statements, supporting indexes and table constraints in generated SQL.
  * Enhanced support for complex schemas, including circular foreign key references and edge cases.

* **Tests**
  * Added comprehensive test coverage for index and constraint SQL generation, complex schema structures, circular references, and error handling.
  * Improved test readability and consistency with new schema factory functions.
  * Updated tests to separate primary key constraints from column definitions into distinct statements for clearer SQL output.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

feat(ci): trigger devin on `chore: version packages`
body:
## Resources

* Successful GHA run from branch with "emulated" `chore: version packages` commit on `HEAD`: https://github.com/reown-com/appkit/actions/runs/15140944047
* E2E validation is [here](https://reown-inc.slack.com/archives/C08MKQ248SY/p1747753072661359) (Devin being triggered via the Slack webhook)

## Description

Introduces a new GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/devin_docs_update.yml`) designed to automate the process of triggering documentation updates.

**Workflow Details:**

*   **Trigger:** The workflow runs on pushes to the `main` and `test/devin-workflow-trigger` branches, specifically when the head commit message contains 'chore: version packages'.
*   **Action:**
    *   It sends a detailed Slack notification into `#system-devin`
    *   The notification prompts an analysis of the commit's impact (focusing on `CHANGELOG.md` files) on the `reown-com/reown-docs` repository.
    *   It provides instructions for creating a pull request in `reown-com/reown-docs` if documentation updates are necessary, or to report if no updates are needed.
*   **Context:** The Slack message includes relevant context such as the repository name, commit SHA, and a direct link to the commit.

**Key Improvements:**

*   **Automation:** Streamlines the process of initiating documentation reviews following version package updates.
*   **Clarity:** Provides clear instructions and context for the documentation update task.
*   **Efficiency:** Ensures that documentation updates are considered promptly after relevant code changes are merged.

**Considerations:**

*   The workflow includes error handling for missing secrets and failed `curl` or `jq` commands.

## Type of change

- [ ] Chore (non-breaking change that addresses non-functional tasks, maintenance, or code quality improvements)
- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [x] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)

# Checklist

- [ ] Code in this PR is covered by automated tests (Unit tests, E2E tests)
- [x] My changes generate no new warnings
- [x] I have reviewed my own code
- [ ] I have filled out all required sections
- [ ] I have tested my changes on the preview link
- [ ] Approver of this PR confirms that the changes are tested on the preview link

title: ✨ add schema validation to createNewVersion function
body:
## Issue - resolve: Add validation to prevent invalid schemas from being created ## Why is this change needed? The `createNewVersion` function was not validating schema structure before creating new versions, which could result in invalid schemas being saved to the database. This enhancement adds proper validation using the `schemaSchema` from `@liam-hq/db-structure` to ensure data integrity. ## What would you like reviewers to focus on? - Schema validation logic implementation - Error handling and message formatting - Integration with existing validation patterns ## Testing Verification Tested locally to ensure validation properly catches invalid schema structures and returns appropriate error messages. ![Pasted_Image_2025_06_11__17_05](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/40b7a6c8-dfa3-4735-a9cd-35795fd4044a) ## What was done ### 🤖 Generated by PR Agent at 04009f807249b2dd4a5e019a83082852fb26d0db • Add schema validation to `createNewVersion` function using `schemaSchema` • Implement error handling with detailed validation messages • Prevent invalid schemas from being saved to database • Return formatted error responses when validation fails ## Detailed Changes

Enhancement

title: 🐛 fix: resolve duplicate user messages and remove dbId field
body:
## Issue - resolve: Fix duplicate user messages being displayed in chat interface ## Why is this change needed? User messages were being duplicated in the chat interface due to issues with the optimistic update mechanism and duplicate detection logic. This created a poor user experience where messages appeared twice in the conversation. ## What would you like reviewers to focus on? - The improved duplicate detection logic in `useRealtimeMessages.ts` - Removal of the `dbId` field and simplification of message identification - Content-based duplicate detection for user messages with timestamp tolerance - Updated optimistic update logic for handling temporary vs persisted message IDs ## Testing Verification - Tested message sending and confirmed no duplicate messages appear - Verified that optimistic updates work correctly - Confirmed that realtime message synchronization functions properly - All linting and build processes pass | before | after | | --- | --- | | | | ## What was done ### 🤖 Generated by PR Agent at 15833a32151ca9aee804bb730a4aab90b014cac8 • Fix duplicate user messages in chat interface • Remove `dbId` field to simplify message identification • Implement content-based duplicate detection with timestamp tolerance • Improve optimistic update logic for temporary vs persisted messages ## Detailed Changes
Relevant files
Bug fix
useRealtimeMessages.ts
Enhanced duplicate detection and optimistic updates           
frontend/apps/app/components/Chat/hooks/useRealtimeMessages.ts • Replace dbId-based duplicate detection with ID and content-based
checking
• Add timestamp tolerance for user message duplicate
detection
• Refactor optimistic update logic to handle temporary vs
persisted message IDs
• Remove dbId field references throughout
message handling code
+50/-25 
chatTypes.ts
Simplify ChatEntry type structure                                               
frontend/apps/app/components/Chat/types/chatTypes.ts • Remove `dbId` field from `ChatEntry` type definition
+0/-2     
## Additional Notes This change simplifies the message handling architecture by removing the `dbId` field and relying on a unified `id` field for message identification. The improved duplicate detection uses content-based matching for user messages, making the system more robust against edge cases that were causing duplicates. ___ >

title: feat(api/ci): idmux
Generate knowledge on feedback resolved
body:
## Why is this change needed? To provide more accurate Knowledge Suggestions, Knowledge Suggestions are now regenerated when severity resolves `CRITICAL`, `WARNING`, and `QUESTION` review feedback. ![ss 3151](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8a7fc235-84e6-416d-87b1-a34cdc4caede) ## What would you like reviewers to focus on? ## Testing Verification ✅ Local check When severity resolves `CRITICAL`, `WARNING`, and `QUESTION` `generate-knowledge-from-feedback` job was triggered. ![ss 3150](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e13b7d52-c222-4e85-8b20-0a3f253ec9a7) ![ss 3149](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5afd9b98-d4c4-435e-be1b-ef9f55015d4f) and `ReviewFeedbackKnowledgeSuggestionMapping` record was created. ![ss 3152](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d8bf9e6d-56b9-422a-839f-449d65162200) When severity resolves `POSITIVE` `generate-knowledge-from-feedback` job was not triggered. ## What was done ### 🤖 Generated by PR Agent at ea9debe46b4507e698506633499202b4e9246a20 - Introduced `ReviewFeedbackKnowledgeSuggestionMapping` table for mapping feedback to knowledge suggestions. - Enhanced feedback resolution to trigger knowledge suggestion generation. - Added logic to fetch and validate feedback data before marking it as resolved. - Triggered knowledge suggestion creation tasks upon feedback resolution. - Updated documentation and schema generation processes to include review feedback context. - Added `reviewFeedback` parameter to various functions for better integration. - Enhanced prompts to include detailed review feedback information. - Fixed handling of `updatedFeedback` to ensure correct knowledge suggestion generation. ## Detailed Changes

Enhancement

title: 🔧 Fix turbo cache for @liam-hq/db-structure#gen task
body:
## Issue - resolve: Fix build failures caused by missing outputs configuration in Turborepo cache ## Why is this change needed? The `@liam-hq/db-structure#gen` task was being cached by Turborepo without proper outputs configuration. This caused subsequent builds to fail with "Cannot find module './schema.generated.js'" errors because the generated files weren't properly tracked in the cache. ## What would you like reviewers to focus on? - Verify the outputs path `src/parser/tbls/schema.generated.ts` is correct - Ensure this resolves the build cache issues in CI/CD ## Testing Verification I ran re-deploy multiple times with Vercel and confirmed that the build passed even with the cache in effect. ref: https://vercel.com/liambx/liam-erd-sample/9goNEgLUWkoTLuX4eoyxMs8mMmRb?turboRunSummary=true&comparisonId=dpl_BQyB9jGDfGPEnXLZsLuKFSF2mT96 ## What was done ### 🤖 Generated by PR Agent at 3e9bbc0feb7bc6b3c080cef901d4c87a9d15d0de • Fix Turborepo cache configuration for `@liam-hq/db-structure#gen` task • Add missing outputs configuration to prevent build failures • Ensure generated schema files are properly tracked in cache ## Detailed Changes
Relevant files
Configuration changes
turbo.json
Add cache outputs for db-structure gen task                           
turbo.json • Add specific cache configuration for @liam-hq/db-structure#gen task

• Configure outputs to track src/parser/tbls/schema.generated.ts file

• Set dependency on ^gen and error-only output logging
+5/-0     
## Additional Notes This fix ensures Turborepo properly caches the generated schema file, preventing build failures in dependent packages. ___ >

title: refactor: use blockchain api if not auth provider when fetching balance
body:
# Description

Updates `getMyTokensWithBalance` method to make sure `getAssets` and `getCapabilities` calls only when using Auth provider. Calling these methods triggers deep links which causing unexpected behaviour. Secondary solution to https://github.com/reown-com/appkit/pull/4418

*since there is no way to disconnect iFrame on the PW tests, this removes the offline balance check tests bc since we are now fetching balance with `wallet_getAssets` even though we set the browser offline, it's still able to get the balances from the embedded wallet.

title: Bump antd from 5.24.0 to 5.24.2
body:
Bumps [antd](https://github.com/ant-design/ant-design) from 5.24.0 to 5.24.2.
Release notes

Sourced from antd's releases.

5.24.2

  • Input
    • 🐞 Fix Input with component token inputFontSize breaks the height of controlHeight. #52865 @​zombieJ
    • 🐞 Fix Input.Search has a border that is not aligned with the bottom of the search button when configure variable as underlined. #52861 @​ustcfury
    • 🛠 Improve Input.OTP logic for create default state. #52878 @​Dandelion-F
    • 🛠 Improve Input.OTP implementation for render separator. #52841 @​li-jia-nan
  • Watermark
    • 🐞 Fix Watermark may cause page unresponsive when re-rendering. #52897 @​765477020
    • 🆕 Improve Watermark rendering logic to avoid disable it via developer tools and hidden attribute. #52891 @​arronlai
  • 🐞 Fix DatePicker.RangePicker arrow position not correctly when sometime reopened. #52854 @​zombieJ
  • 🐞 Fix Layout.Sider content overflow issue when collapsedWidth={0}. #52862 @​afc163
  • 🛠 Refactor Grid internal useBreakpoint logic to be same as other component, this will not affect usage. #52870 @​zombieJ
  • 💄 Fix Button styles for hyperlink mode. #52888 @​DDDDD12138
  • 💄 Fix Table sortable column headers could not wrap automatically. #52899 @​765477020
  • ⚡️ Improve Menu re-rendering performance when pass function to expandIcon property. #52863 @​wanpan11
  • ⚡️ Improve Carousel indicator animation performance. #52881 @​li-jia-nan
  • RTL
    • 💄 Fix DatePicker wrong icon direction for RTL mode. #52896 @​li-jia-nan
    • 💄 Fix Dropdown wrong arrow direction of multi-level menu for RTL mode. #52885 @​yellowryan
  • Input
    • 🐞 修复 Input 配置 inputFontSize component token 时，controlHeight 会不生效的问题。#52865 @​zombieJ
    • 🐞 修复 Input.Search 在设置 variant 为 underlined 时下边框与搜索按钮底部没对齐的问题。#52861 @​ustcfury
    • 🛠 优化 Input.OTP 的默认状态创建逻辑。#52878 @​Dandelion-F
    • 🛠 优化 Input.OTP 的分隔符渲染实现。#52841 @​li-jia-nan
  • Watermark
    • 🐞 修复 Watermark 重新渲染时可能导致页面卡死的问题。#52897 @​765477020
    • 🆕 调整 Watermark 渲染逻辑，防止通过开发者工具添加 hidden 属性来去掉水印。#52891 @​arronlai
  • 🐞 修复 DatePicker.RangePicker 在弹层重新打开的时候，有可能出现箭头位置不正确的问题。#52854 @​zombieJ
  • 🐞 修复 Layout.Sider 当 collapsedWidth={0} 时的内容溢出的问题。#52862 @​afc163
  • 🛠 重构 Grid 内部响应式逻辑以复用其他组件类似的逻辑，该更新不会于使用上有所变化。#52870 @​zombieJ
  • 💄 修复 Button 超链接模式的样式。#52888 @​DDDDD12138
  • 💄 修复 Table 可排序列头不自动换行的问题。#52899 @​765477020
  • ⚡️ 优化 Menu 在 expandIcon 属性传入函数时重新渲染的性能。#52863 @​wanpan11
  • ⚡️ 优化 Carousel 指示器的动画性能。#52881 @​li-jia-nan
  • RTL
    • 💄 修复 DatePicker 在 RTL 模式下图标方向错误的问题。#52896 @​li-jia-nan
    • 💄 修复 Dropdown 在 RTL 模式下多级菜单箭头方向错误的问题。#52885 @​yellowryan

5.24.1

  • 🐞 Fix Button with color to be primary and variant to be text or link will not use correct color. #52812 @​zombieJ
  • 💄 Fix Input.Group & Input.OTP style issues caused by undefined CSS variables. #52799 @​afc163
  • 🐞 Fix DatePicker with long content prefix breaks the layout. #52776 @​guoyunhe
  • 🐞 Fix Table title missing aria-label when sorting. #52772 @​mellis481
  • 🐞 Fix Alert.ErrorBoundary type error when used as a JSX component with @types/react@18.x. #52766 @​afc163
  • 💄 Fix Segmented shape not working with size. #52757 @​yellowryan
  • 🐞 修复 Button 配置 color 为 primary 并且 variant 为 text 或 link 时，没有取用正确的色板的问题。#52812 @​zombieJ
  • 💄 修复 Input.Group 与 Input.OTP 由于 css 变量未定义导致样式异常的问题。#52799 @​afc163
  • 🐞 修复 DatePicker 的 prefix 内容多时会换行的问题。#52776 @​guoyunhe

... (truncated)

Changelog

Sourced from antd's changelog.

5.24.2

2025-02-24

  • Input
    • 🐞 Fix Input with component token inputFontSize breaks the height of controlHeight. #52865 @​zombieJ
    • 🐞 Fix Input.Search has a border that is not aligned with the bottom of the search button when configure variable as underlined. #52861 @​ustcfury
    • 🛠 Improve Input.OTP logic for create default state. #52878 @​Dandelion-F
    • 🛠 Improve Input.OTP implementation for render separator. #52841 @​li-jia-nan
  • Watermark
    • 🐞 Fix Watermark may cause page unresponsive when re-rendering. #52897 @​765477020
    • 🆕 Improve Watermark rendering logic to avoid disable it via developer tools and hidden attribute. #52891 @​arronlai
  • 🐞 Fix DatePicker.RangePicker arrow position not correctly when sometime reopened. #52854 @​zombieJ
  • 🐞 Fix Layout.Sider content overflow issue when collapsedWidth={0}. #52862 @​afc163
  • 🛠 Refactor Grid internal useBreakpoint logic to be same as other component, this will not affect usage. #52870 @​zombieJ
  • 💄 Fix Button styles for hyperlink mode. #52888 @​DDDDD12138
  • 💄 Fix Table sortable column headers could not wrap automatically. #52899 @​765477020
  • ⚡️ Improve Menu re-rendering performance when pass function to expandIcon property. #52863 @​wanpan11
  • ⚡️ Improve Carousel indicator animation performance. #52881 @​li-jia-nan
  • RTL
    • 💄 Fix DatePicker wrong icon direction for RTL mode. #52896 @​li-jia-nan
    • 💄 Fix Dropdown wrong arrow direction of multi-level menu for RTL mode. #52885 @​yellowryan

5.24.1

2025-02-17

  • 🐞 Fix Button with color to be primary and variant to be text or link will not use correct color. #52812 @​zombieJ
  • 💄 Fix Input.Group & Input.OTP style issues caused by undefined CSS variables. #52799 @​afc163
  • 🐞 Fix DatePicker with long content prefix breaks the layout. #52776 @​guoyunhe
  • 🐞 Fix Table title missing aria-label when sorting. #52772 @​mellis481
  • 🐞 Fix Alert.ErrorBoundary type error when used as a JSX component with @types/react@18.x. #52766 @​afc163
  • 💄 Fix Segmented shape not working with size. #52757 @​yellowryan
Commits
  • d78cd5c docs: changelog for 5.24.2 (#52935)
  • db284a4 fix Issue52048 Watermark Component Re-render Causes Page Freeze (#52897)
  • ea29898 demo: update demo (#52924)
  • 7134c5d docs: docs update (#52918)
  • cf2b6e4 chore(deps-dev): bump pixelmatch in the dev-dependencies group (#52914)
  • 8a3c690 demo(form): correct disabled styles (#52912)
  • 3b5b2a0 chore: enable react-scan in development (#52910)
  • 883b17f docs: update App FAQ (#52904)
  • 18d6384 chore: update version support text (#52900)
  • 6eebde3 fix(Table):The single word column title does not wrap for sortable column (#5...
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Added explicit client names to gRPC client instantiations across the system for improved identification and logging.
  - Introduced a new error for missing gRPC server streams and enhanced nil stream handling to prevent runtime errors.
  - Added hardware and system resource information output to GitHub Actions for improved workflow diagnostics.
  - Added a new configuration option for HPA enablement in gateway load balancer development values.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Improved locking strategy and error handling in test and client code to prevent concurrency issues and invalid stream usage.
  - Fixed and streamlined workflow triggers in CI/CD pipelines for more accurate build and test execution.

- **Refactor**
  - Simplified and improved connection management and logging in gRPC client and pool logic.
  - Updated Makefile and related scripts for more robust file handling, formatting, and permission management.

- **Chores**
  - Updated and migrated GolangCI-Lint configuration to JSON format and removed obsolete YAML config.
  - Updated dependency versions and version files for tools and libraries.

- **Tests**
  - Enhanced test scaffolding and parameterization for improved coverage and maintainability.
  - Increased logging verbosity in end-to-end test configurations for better debugging.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: ✨ Add TableGroupNode when creating a bounding box on ERDContent
body:
I’ve updated the interaction experience for creating a `TableGroupNode`. (Previously: Users selected multiple `TableNodes` and clicked the `GroupButton` to create a group.) - The `GroupButton` is now a toggle button that switches the TableGroup operation mode on or off. - When the TableGroup operation mode is **on**, drawing a bounding box on the `ERDContent` will create a `TableGroupNode`. ## What would you like reviewers to focus on? ## Testing Verification https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4a9d6140-5a11-491a-ac66-963718447c0c ## What was done ### 🤖 Generated by PR Agent at 9b5f0945296ec80cd32228c3a944a7b8950f50f4 - Introduced a bounding box feature for grouping tables. - Added `TableGroupBoundingBox` component for visualizing the bounding box. - Implemented `useTableGroupBoundingBox` hook for managing bounding box interactions. - Updated `GroupButton` to toggle table group editing mode. - Enhanced `Toolbar` and related components to support the new table grouping functionality. - Modified `ERDContent` and `ERDRenderer` to integrate table group creation via bounding box. ## Detailed Changes

https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/issues/177.

- `anonymous_helper_widget_access` feature flag controls the global roll out.
- query param `?anonymous_helper_widget_access=true` controls individual access

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
	- Enabled anonymous access to the helper widget on select pages, allowing users without accounts to interact with the widget where permitted.
- **Refactor**
	- Simplified and unified the helper widget’s initialization data, improving maintainability and client-side integration.
- **Bug Fixes**
	- Improved access control logic for displaying the helper widget, ensuring it appears only when appropriate.

  • 💄 修复 DatePicker 头部按钮意外间距导致的未对齐问题。#53007 @​DDDDD12138

title: Fixed incorrect workflow file
body:
## Issue

- resolve: Workflow file syntax error introduced in #2208

## Why is this change needed?
PR #2208 introduced incorrect syntax in the claude.yml workflow file that would prevent it from running properly:
- `timeout-minutes` was set as a string ("60") instead of a number (60)
- There was a duplicate `timeout_minutes` parameter in the claude-code-action configuration

## What would you like reviewers to focus on?
Please verify that the workflow file syntax is now correct and the timeout values are properly configured.

## Testing Verification
- Checked the workflow file syntax
- Confirmed that `timeout-minutes` should be a number according to GitHub Actions documentation
- Removed duplicate timeout configuration from the action parameters

## What was done
Fixed the claude.yml workflow file by:
1. Changed `timeout-minutes: "60"` to `timeout-minutes: 60` (removed quotes to make it a number)
2. Removed the duplicate `timeout_minutes: "60"` parameter from the claude-code-action

## Detailed Changes
- `.github/workflows/claude.yml`: Fixed timeout configuration syntax

## Additional Notes
This fixes the syntax errors that were introduced in PR #2208.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Updated internal workflow configuration to improve reliability. No user-facing changes.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

### What

- Add `cancelled_at` column to `equity_grants` table

TODO:

- cancel equity grant on contract end

### Why
Ease the process of cancelling equity grants/forfeiting options.

title: Don't generate S3 download URLs ahead of time
body:
Currently, the `/documents` page is slow in part because we pre-generate S3 download URLs for every single row. This PR removes this generation and instead adds an endpoint to explicitly redirect to the download.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Added a secure download route for file attachments, allowing users to download files via a new URL format.

- **Improvements**
  - Updated all download links for attachments and documents to use the new secure download route, enhancing consistency and security.
  - Improved the display and conditional rendering of download buttons across billing, cap table uploads, documents, invoices, and tender offers.
  - Adjusted form integration and display logic on the tender offers page for better usability.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Ensured download links are only shown when attachments exist.

- **Tests**
  - Extended end-to-end tests to verify the presence and correctness of download links for document attachments.

- **Chores**
  - Updated TypeScript configuration to target ECMAScript 2024 for improved language support.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: fix(mode-governance): add token approval check before staking tokens
body: # Relates to: # Background ## What does this PR do? This PR fixes an error in the Mode Governance plugin by adding a check—and if necessary, a token approval transaction—before performing the staking via the createLock function. This improvement prevents the "ERC20InsufficientAllowance" error when staking MODE or BPT tokens without previously granting the escrow contract permission. # Testing To test these changes, please follow these steps: 1. Navigate to the examples directory and use an example that stakes MODE or BPT tokens. 2. Import and instantiate the ModeGovernanceService plugin. 3. Execute the stake action (executeGovernanceStake) with an amount greater than the current allowance to verify that the approve transaction is executed first, followed by the createLock transaction. ## Detailed testing results | Method | Prompt | Screenshot | Transaction Link | |----------|--------|-------|------------------| | executeGovernanceStake | Stake 10 MODE tokens (minimal required is 100) | | n/a | | executeGovernanceStake | Stake 100 MODE tokens | | [tx](https://explorer.mode.network/tx/0x9dd953c5e5aca8e6eec8c74e788475b98391112c99df828d21ddc226b9698cad) | | Governance Mode Website | n/a | | n/a | # Docs My changes do not require a change to the project documentation. ## Checklist - [x] I have tested this change and added the relevant screenshots to the PR description - [ ] I updated the [README](https://github.com/goat-sdk/goat/blob/main/README.md) if necessary to include the new plugin, wallet, chain, etc.

This pull request introduces a new script to check for high-risk changes and integrates this script into the CI workflow. The script assesses the risk level of changes based on the number of lines changed and the type of content modified. The most important changes include the addition of the script itself and the updates to the workflow configuration to run this script.

### High-risk change detection:

* [`.github/scripts/check-low-risk-changes.sh`](diffhunk://#diff-9e0871d4f351e0091481aae2783622c462547cbdcf7a41971f3f5c7b601d78dfR1-R133): Added a new script to determine if changes in a pull request are high-risk. The script checks the number of lines changed and analyzes the content of the changes to identify potentially complex code modifications.

title: fix(mcp): Change readRepomixOutputTool response from resource to text
body:
<!-- Please include a summary of the changes -->

Changed the response type of the MCP tool (`readRepomixOutputTool`) from `resource` to `text`.

related:

- https://github.com/yamadashy/repomix/issues/472

## Background / Reason

In the Cursor environment, MCP tool responses of type `resource` are not properly recognized or displayed. To ensure compatibility and correct display, the response is now returned as `text`.

- [x] Run `npm run test`
- [x] Run `npm run lint`

title: Balmy Plugin
body:
# Relates to:
Integration of Balmy Protocol into GOAT SDK ecosystem

Issue: Add support for Balmy DCA protocol

# Background

## What does this PR do?
- Adds a new plugin (@goat-sdk/plugin-balmy) to integrate with Balmy Protocol
- Implements core functionality for token balance checking, allowance management, and swap execution
- Provides built-in permit2 support for gasless approvals
- Enables multi-chain support for DCA operations

## What kind of change is this?
- [x] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [x] New plugin integration
- [x] Documentation addition

# Documentation changes needed?
My changes require a change to the project documentation:
- Added new README.md for the Balmy plugin
- Need to add Balmy plugin to main GOAT SDK documentation
- Need to add example implementation in vercel-ai examples

# Testing

## Detailed testing steps

1. Test Balance Checking
```typescript
const balances = await plugin.getBalance({
    account: "0x...",
    tokens: {
        1: ["0xa0b86991c6218b36c1d19d4a2e9eb0ce3606eb48"]
    }
});
// Verify balance retrieval works across chains
```

2. Test Allowance Checking
```typescript
const allowances = await plugin.getAllowance({
    chainId: 1,
    token: "0xa0b86991c6218b36c1d19d4a2e9eb0ce3606eb48",
    owner: "0x...",
    spenders: ["0x..."]
});
// Verify allowance data is correct
```

3. Test Quote Retrieval
```typescript
const quotes = await plugin.getQuote({
    chainId: 1,
    sellToken: "0xa0b86991c6218b36c1d19d4a2e9eb0ce3606eb48",
    buyToken: "0xc02aaa39b223fe8d0a0e5c4f27ead9083c756cc2",
    order: {
        type: "sell",
        sellAmount: "1000000"
    },
    slippagePercentage: 1
});
// Verify quotes are returned with valid data
```

4. Test Swap Execution
```typescript
const tx = await plugin.executeSwap({
    chainId: 1,
    sellToken: "0xa0b86991c6218b36c1d19d4a2e9eb0ce3606eb48",
    buyToken: "0xc02aaa39b223fe8d0a0e5c4f27ead9083c756cc2",
    order: {
        type: "sell",
        sellAmount: "1000000"
    },
    slippagePercentage: 1
});
// Verify transaction executes successfully
```

## For plugins
- [x] I have tested this change locally with key pair wallets
- [] I have tested this change locally with hosted wallets (e.g. Crossmint Smart Wallets, etc.)

## Discord username - 0xkamal7

Additional Things that have been in WIP:
- The plugin to be tested on multiple EVM chains including Ethereum mainnet and Optimism
- All core functionality has been verified with both EOA and smart contract wallets
- Permit2 integration has been tested for gasless approvals
- Error handling has been implemented for all major failure cases
- Type definitions are complete and properly exported

title: feat: top level functions
body:
## 📝 Summary

Adds top level functions

 - `@app.fn` (but maybe `app.function` is better) to wrap functions, register them but leave them directly exposed
 - Serialize to `@app.fn` if there are no dependencies
 
 Just an intro PR for #2293

For completion of 2293, I think out standing changes are:
 - [ ] some UI aspect (maybe a new language toggle?)
 - [ ] Top level imports
 - [ ] Looser requirements for serialization (can use imports and other app.fns)

But this is hidden under `App(_toplevel_fn=True)`

## 🔍 Description of Changes

see the smoke test or:

```python
@app.fn
def self_ref_fib(n: int) -> int:
    if n == 0:
        return 0
    if n == 1:
        return 1
    return self_ref_fib(n - 1) + self_ref_fib(n - 2)


@app.fn(disabled=True, hide_code=True)
def divide(x, y):
    return y / x


@app.fn
def subtraction(a: "int", b: "int") -> "int":
    return a - b


@app.fn
# Comments inbetween
def multiply(a, b) -> "int":
    return a * b


@app.fn
def addition(a: int, b: int) -> int:
    # int is considered no good, re-eval
    return a + b
```

title: feat: integrate LangGraph into streaming workflow with hybrid architecture
body:
## Why is this change needed? The current workflow (`streamingWorkflow.ts`) was not using LangGraph, so it has been modified to use it.🙏 Enable LangGraph usage in streaming workflows while maintaining real-time streaming capabilities through a hybrid architecture approach.（I also did some refactoring of the file structure, etc.） The file structure under `frontend/internal-packages/agent/src/chat/workflow/services` and `shared/ `is as follows ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "shared/ - Common components" LGU[langGraphUtils.ts] SM[stateManager.ts] end subgraph "services/ - Workflow Implementation" NSW[nonStreamingWorkflow.ts] SW[streamingWorkflow.ts] WS[workflowSteps.ts] end NSW --> LGU NSW --> SM SW --> LGU SW --> SM SW --> WS WS --> SM ``` ## What would you like reviewers to focus on? ## Testing Verification ✅ I have verified that I can chat with the agent correctly. ## What was done ### 🤖 Generated by PR Agent at 6de23ba756c510e3d251be71cbcca9f62c0a05f4 - Refactored workflow services for LangGraph integration and code clarity - Migrated streaming workflow to use LangGraph with hybrid streaming architecture - Consolidated shared logic into new shared utility files - Removed unused types, functions, and legacy step logic - Improved maintainability and readability of workflow codebase ## Detailed Changes

Update import for streaming workflow to new services path

title: fix: Downgrades typescript to 5.7.2

If N/A, write N/A here and check the checkbox. - [ ] I confirm automated tests are in place that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works. ## How should this be tested? 1. title: DB-backed Gumroad blog
body:
This is a little bit scrappy -- but it should be a good starting point for us to get a sense of how we like this approach.

I will roll this out to prod behind a feature flag so we can leverage the full catalog of existing posts. I will also follow up with another change to seed the dev environment with enough meaningful data.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Introduced a public-facing Gumroad Blog with index and post pages, accessible via a new "Blog" link in the navigation and footer when enabled.
  - Blog posts display featured images, publication dates, and support rich text with call-to-action buttons.
  - Responsive, accessible blog layout with featured, recent, and grid-style post sections.
  - Anonymous users can view blog posts if permitted.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Improved navigation and footer to conditionally display the "Blog" link based on user access.

- **Style**
  - Added scoped Tailwind CSS styling for the blog to ensure consistent appearance.

- **Tests**
  - Added comprehensive tests for blog access policies, controller actions, and featured image extraction.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: ✨ enhance the schema.rb parser to support Constraints
body:
## Issue - resolve: ## Why is this change needed? We introduced constraints of schema and update tbls and Prisma parser to support the feature. This PR will update schema.rb parser to support constraints. ### ref - introduce constraints and update tbls parser https://github.com/liam-hq/liam/pull/1168 - update Prisma parser https://github.com/liam-hq/liam/pull/1386 ## What would you like reviewers to focus on? - the constraints are parsed properly - The schema.rb parser will support "PRIMARY KEY", "FOREIGN KEY", "UNIQUE" and "CHECK" constraints. - the tests are appropriate ## Testing Verification - About the parser, I updated some test cases - About the application, I checked the behaviour in the following steps 1. copy the following schema and save on your local PC (that's a slight modified copy of [this file](https://github.com/liam-hq/liam/blob/f20e860c311dcc0f2b05b68a921f0faa3101a346/frontend/packages/db-structure/src/parser/schemarb/input/schema1.in.rb)) 1. run `pnpm run build --filter @liam-hq/cli` to build cli 1. run `node frontend/packages/cli/dist-cli/bin/cli.js erd build --input schema.rb` to generate scripts 1. run `npx http-server dist` to run the application and go to http://localhost:8080/ 1. click "users" table and see the Constraints section of its table details ![Screenshot 0007-04-22 at 21 18 12](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/92612b7e-1f3b-4b42-90d0-100165ca7f16)

Each company is a top-level entity that owns departments, users, and projects.", force: :cascade do |t| t.string "name", null: false, comment: "The name of the company." t.string "address", null: false, comment: "The registered address of the company." end create_table "departments", id: :uuid, default: -> { "gen_random_uuid()" }, comment: "Represents departments within a company, organizing users into functional groups.", force: :cascade do |t| t.string "name", null: false, comment: "The name of the department." t.uuid "company_id", null: false, comment: "Foreign key linking the department to a company." t.index ["company_id"], name: "index_departments_on_company_id" end create_table "users", id: :uuid, default: -> { "gen_random_uuid()" }, comment: "Represents employees or members of a company, who are assigned roles and tasks.", force: :cascade do |t| t.uuid "company_id", null: false, comment: "Foreign key linking the user to a company." t.uuid "department_id", null: false, comment: "Foreign key linking the user to a department." t.datetime "created_at", null: false, comment: "The timestamp when the user was created." t.string "name", default: "new user", null: true, comment: "The user's full name." t.string "email", null: false, comment: "The user's email address." t.integer "age", default: 30, null: true, comment: "The user's age." t.boolean "is_deleted", ["company_id"], name: "index_users_on_company_id" t.index ["department_id"], name: "index_users_on_department_id" t.index ["email"], name: "index_users_on_email", unique: true end create_table "roles", id: :uuid, default: -> { "gen_random_uuid()" }, comment: "Defines roles that can be assigned to users, such as 'Admin' or 'Manager'.", force: :cascade do |t| t.string "name", null: false, comment: "The name of the role." t.string "description", comment: "A brief description of the role's purpose or permissions." end create_table "user_roles", id: :uuid, default: -> { comment: "Foreign key linking to the user." t.uuid "project_id", null: false, comment: "Foreign key linking to the project." t.index ["user_id", "project_id"], unique: true, name: "index_project_assignments_on_user_id_and_project_id", comment: "Ensures that each user is uniquely assigned to a project." end create_table "tasks", id: :uuid, default: -> { "gen_random_uuid()" }, (e.g., 0: pending, 1: in progress, 2: completed)." t.index ["project_id"], name: "index_tasks_on_project_id" t.index ["assigned_user_id"], name: "index_tasks_on_assigned_user_id" end create_table "comments", id: :uuid, default: -> { "gen_random_uuid()" }, comment: "Stores comments on tasks, enabling discussions or updates.", force: :cascade do |t| t.text "content", null: false, comment: "The "index_comments_on_user_id" end create_table "timesheets", id: :uuid, default: -> { "gen_random_uuid()" }, comment: "Tracks time spent by users on tasks for reporting or billing purposes.", force: "duration_minutes", null: false, comment: "The total duration of work in minutes." t.index ["user_id"], name: "index_timesheets_on_user_id" t.index ["task_id"], name: "index_timesheets_on_task_id" end ## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **新機能**
  - チャットメッセージに一意のIDが付与されるようになりました。
  - メッセージの追加・更新をID単位で柔軟に行える新機能が追加されました。
  - メッセージID生成のユーティリティ関数を追加しました。

- **改善**
  - AI応答やコードブロックの処理が文章単位で細かくリアルタイムに反映されるようになりました。
  - メッセージ保存が複数件まとめて効率的に行われるように改善されました。
  - メッセージ結合の条件が厳密化され、内容管理がより正確になりました。
  - チャットログの保存処理がバッチ化され、無駄な保存を削減しました。
  - ストリーミング処理の音声合成や状態管理を改善し安定性を向上しました。
  - YouTubeコメント処理のフローを簡素化しました。

- **バグ修正**
  - 保存APIのログファイル読み込み時のエラー処理とデータ整合性を強化しました。

- **リファクタ**
  - チャット処理のテキスト解析やメッセージ管理をモジュール化し、保守性を向上しました。
  - 不要な変数や関数を削除しコードを整理しました。

✨️ feat: enhance GitHub session form UI with projects & branches dropdowns
body:
## Issue - resolve: ## Why is this change needed? This change significantly improves the UX of GitHubSessionFormPresenter by replacing native select elements with custom dropdown components that include icons and visual information. The update makes project and branch selection more intuitive and adds visual feedback for pending/error states, enhancing overall form usability. ## What would you like reviewers to focus on? 1. Custom dropdown implementations: Whether the UI/UX of ProjectsDropdown and BranchesDropdown is appropriate 2. Component reusability: The shared ProjectIcon component and dropdown component design patterns 3. Visual effects: Implementation of gradient borders and shadow effects for pending/error states 4. Storybook stories: Completeness of interactive stories covering all component states ## Testing Verification https://liam-storybook-git-feat-github-session-form-styling-liambx.vercel.app/?path=/docs/features-sessions-githubsessionformpresenter--docs https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d46af16-5116-4c38-ade3-78ffb33561ae ## What was done ### 🤖 Generated by PR Agent at 5c9713fdbe880cdffb6be16276877c2c6c56b44f - Replace native select elements with custom dropdown components - Add visual states for pending/error form conditions - Create reusable ProjectIcon and Code components - Implement comprehensive Storybook documentation ## Detailed Changes
